items.title: Steijn dreigt Europa League-duel van Feyenoord met Real Betis te missen
items.summary: Feyenoord kan donderdag in het Europa League-duel met Real Betis naar alle waarschijnlijkheid geen beroep doen op Sem Steijn. De aanvallende middenvelder heeft op de afsluitende training van de Rotterdammers een nog onbekende blessure opgelopen.

items.title: Waarom Celtic op zijn hoede is voor 'heel gevaarlijk' FC Utrecht
items.summary: Voor de tweede keer dit Europese seizoen staat Celtic tegenover een Nederlandse tegenstander. Na het bezoek aan Feyenoord wacht donderdag een thuisduel met FC Utrecht. 'Misschien zijn ze in eigen land de weg kwijtgeraakt, maar ze kunnen absoluut voetballen.'

items.title: Arbeloa: Bellingham 'cornerstone' of Madrid future
items.summary: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa praised Jude Bellingham on Tuesday, calling the midfielder "a leader" who will be a "cornerstone of this team for many years."

items.title: Fury's comeback fight confirmed
items.summary: Tyson Fury has confirmed his comeback fight against Aslanbek Makhmudov as the former heavyweight champion returns to boxing.

items.title: Meer dan een romantische stap: waarom Remko Pasveer terugkeert bij Heracles
items.summary: Heracles Almelo tovert in de laatste week van de transferperiode een verrassing uit de hoge hoed. Remko Pasveer keert na twaalf jaar terug in het Asito Stadion. De 42-jarige doelman komt over van Ajax en wil de Almeloërs helpen om in de Eredivisie te blijven. De statistieken tonen aan dat Heracles de bak aan ervaring van Pasveer goed kan gebruiken.

items.title: Pellegrini vindt Feyenoord 'Champions League-waardig' en licht Antony uit
items.summary: Real Betis-trainer Manuel Pellegrini heeft zich in aanloop naar de Europa League-ontmoeting met Feyenoord lovend uitgelaten over de huidige nummer twee van de Eredivisie. De Chileense coach ging ook in op de rol van de afgelopen weekend veelbesproken ex-Ajacied Antony binnen zijn ploeg.

items.title: 'In aanvallend opzicht een echte Driouech-wedstrijd'
items.summary: In de nieuwe aflevering van Rondje Europa bespreken Pieter Zwart, Bas van den Hoven en Bart Frouws de kansen van PSV tegen Bayern München, met speciale aandacht voor Couhaib Driouech.

items.title: Abraham joins Villa 1 day after Besiktas transfer
items.summary: Tammy Abraham has signed for Aston Villa from Besiktas just one day after he joined the Turkish club a permanent transfer from Roma.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal had 'beautiful' meeting after loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed he called his players for a meeting to "bring down the temperature" in the wake of their defeat to Manchester United.

items.title: Chelsea urge 'extreme caution' to fans in Naples
items.summary: Chelsea have told fans to take "extreme caution" in Naples after two supporters were treated in hospital ahead of their Champions League tie in the Italian city.

items.title: Rodman, Hayes viral celebration caps off U.S. rout
items.summary: Trinity Rodman capped off the USWNT's 5-0 rout of Chile by coaxing coach Emma Hayes into a sideline dance, as a young American squad secured its fifth consecutive shutout Tuesday night.

items.title: Match of the Day scores viewing figures success with BBC’s new digital strategy
items.summary: Highlight clips available at 8pm on Saturday for first time Traditional viewing of weekend shows has declined Match of the Day’s viewing figures have increased considerably this season in a vindication of the BBC’s new digital strategy. Traditional linear viewing of the Saturday and Sunday evening highlights programmes had declined by 770,000 to 6.88 million average weekly views last month compared with December 2024, but MOTD has gained 1.52 million viewers by making highlight clips available on its website and app at 8pm on Saturdays for the first time. The combined viewing figures for MOTD on iPlayer covering the evening programmes and highlights clips had risen to 61.9 million by the end of December, a big increase on the 45.4 million views by the same period last season. The digital consumption of MOTD has almost doubled since the 2022-23 season, when the total figure by December was 33.1 million. Continue reading...

items.title: West Ham agree James Ward-Prowse loan to Burnley and sign Adama Traoré
items.summary: Midfielder has not featured under Nuno Winger joins from Fulham until June Burnley are poised to sign James Ward-Prowse on loan from West Ham. The midfielder is undergoing a medical with a view to spending the rest of the season at Turf Moor. He is preparing to leave West Ham on a day when the club confirmed the arrival of Adama Traoré from Fulham for a small undisclosed fee. The 30-year-old winger is understood to have signed until June. Ward-Prowse has been out of favour at West Ham since the appointment of Nuno Espírito Santo as manager in September. The former Southampton player was cut from the first-team squad and has not featured under Nuno. Ward-Prowse had a similar experience during a loan at Nottingham Forest last season. He quickly dropped out of Nuno’s plans and was sent back to West Ham early. Continue reading...

items.title: West Ham sign winger Traore from Fulham
items.summary: West Ham sign winger Adama Traore from Fulham on a permanent basis for an undisclosed fee.

items.title: Aki facing disciplinary committee and omitted from Ireland squad
items.summary: Bundee Aki looks set to miss Ireland's opening Six Nations match against France as he was replaced in the squad due to a disciplinary issue.

items.title: Forest goalkeeper Victor ruled out for rest of season
items.summary: Nottingham Forest goalkeeper John Victor is ruled out for the rest of the season with a knee injury.

items.title: Henderson hopeful of Sir Gino returning home next week
items.summary: Sir Gino could return home to Seven Barrows next week following his injury at Cheltenham Trials Day, Nicky Henderson has told Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Belichick 'snubbed by Pro Football Hall of Fame' in first year of eligibility
items.summary: Six-time Super Bowl champion head coach Bill Belichick didn't get voted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame in his first year of eligibility, according to a report from ESPN.

items.title: ‘In our DNA’: Celtic deepen London ties with girls’ football initiative
items.summary: On a soaked Brixton pitch, the club launch their latest programme as part of a widening mission that now stretches from Glasgow’s soup kitchens to Gaza relief You would not expect to find coaches from the Celtic FC Foundation in Brixton. But even the torrential rain in south London has not stopped them and four local teams from turning out to help launch a programme that will provide girls and young women from underprivileged backgrounds in the local area with a chance to play football. It is one of several initiatives established since the foundation began working in London to mark Celtic’s 125th anniversary in 2013. Another, based in Hackney, called Breaking Barriers helps integrate refugee and asylum-seeking communities through the sport. Continue reading...

items.title: Ward-Prowse set for West Ham departure after not playing under Nuno
items.summary: West Ham midfielder James Ward-Prowse is expected to undergo a medical with Burnley on Wednesday as he closes in on a loan move.
